summary: ENVELOPE: PASS — All 4 fields match (change_type=feature, architectural_flag=false, doctrine_version=2.8.0, work_packet_id FK valid). SCOPE: PASS — modified_paths=['migrations/2026-02-24-lcs-spine-v0-skeleton.sql'] is subset of allowed_paths=['migrations/']. No protected assets touched. EXECUTION: FAIL — Cantonal pattern violation (backbone.md §2). The LCS sub-hub creates 4 tables (movement_type_registry, cid_intake, sid_registry, mid_ledger) but lacks the required ERROR table. Constitutional doctrine mandates exactly 1 CANONICAL + 1 ERROR per sub-hub. No existing sub-hub operates without an error table (CT has company_target_errors, DOL has dol_errors, Blog has blog_errors, People has people_errors). The migration must include lcs.lcs_errors with the standard error columns (error_type, error_stage, retry_strategy) and CHECK constraints matching the established pattern. Additionally, the WP must designate which table is CANONICAL — if the three-table CID/SID/MID structure intentionally deviates from single-CANONICAL, an ADR is required with architectural elevation. SECONDARY FINDINGS (also blocking): CTB registry registration of all new tables is required per Registry-First Enforcement (backbone.md §4) — tables must exist in ctb.table_registry before creation. NON-BLOCKING: (1) Dispatch state skip (MINTED->DELIVERED possible). (2) RLS not enabled.
